era_context: Middle Bronze Age in the Levant and Egypt's Second Intermediate Period. Hittite Old Kingdom in Anatolia. Indus Valley Civilization at or near peak. Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia declining; Kassite Babylon rising. Early Shang dynasty in China.

events.title: No specific documented events
events.description: 1978 BCE falls within a period of sparse historical documentation. No specific events are attested in surviving sources for this year. The year is assigned to the broader era context based on archaeological chronology, but no particular political, military, or cultural events are recorded.
events.certainty_note: The absence of documented events is itself the historical fact for this year.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified. The historical record for 1978 BCE is essentially empty.
historiographic_note: 1978 BCE is a year for which no historical events are documented in any surviving source. The year falls within a period of extremely sparse written documentation. Our knowledge of this era comes almost entirely from archaeology, which provides chronological frameworks and evidence of material culture but not year-by-year historical narratives. The assignment of this year to the Middle Bronze Age is based on radiocarbon dating and stratigraphic analysis, not on contemporary written records.
